methods.description: Procedure for obtaining pollen samples from herbarium specimens and preparing them using acetolysis for subsequent microscopic analysis. Includes a specific treatment for difficult material.

steps.description: Twenty-five samples of pollen grains were obtained from herbarium specimens deposited in the IBUG (Instituto de Botánica de la Universidad de Guadalajara) and XAL (Instituto de Ecología, A. C.) herbaria.
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain pollen samples from specified herbarium collections.

steps.description: Pollen grains were acetolyzed according to the methodology of Erdtman (1960).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform acetolysis on pollen grains using the Erdtman method.

steps.description: for difficult material in which compounds formed thin coats on the grains that interfered during the scanning process, the suggestions of Fonnegra (1989) were implemented. The grains were immersed in glacial acetic acid for 24 hours before acetolysis
steps.searchable_summary: Pre-treat difficult pollen material by immersing in glacial acetic acid for 24 hours.
outputs: Pollen grains pre-treated with glacial acetic acid

steps.description: and then transferred to the acetolysis mixture for 1 to 6 hours and the temperature of The following pollen measurements were obtained from 25 grains per sample:
steps.searchable_summary: Transfer pollen grains (pre-treated or not) to acetolysis mixture for 1 to 6 hours.

methods.description: Procedure for obtaining quantitative measurements of specific pollen morphological characters from acetolyzed grains using light microscopy.

steps.description: The following pollen measurements were obtained from 25 grains per sample:
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain quantitative measurements from 25 acetolyzed pollen grains per sample.

steps.description: polar axis
steps.searchable_summary: Measure the polar axis of pollen grains.

steps.description: equatorial diameter
steps.searchable_summary: Measure the equatorial diameter of pollen grains.

steps.description: exine thickness
steps.searchable_summary: Measure the exine thickness of pollen grains.

steps.description: colpus length
steps.searchable_summary: Measure the colpus length of pollen grains.

steps.description: colpus width
steps.searchable_summary: Measure the colpus width of pollen grains.

steps.description: ora width
steps.searchable_summary: Measure the ora width of pollen grains.

steps.description: ora length
steps.searchable_summary: Measure the ora length of pollen grains.

steps.description: spinae length
steps.searchable_summary: Measure the spinae length of pollen grains.

steps.description: spine width at base
steps.searchable_summary: Measure the spine width at the base of pollen grains.
outputs: Spine width at base measurement values

steps.description: and number of apertures.
steps.searchable_summary: Count the number of apertures on pollen grains.

methods.description: Procedure for preparing acetolyzed pollen grains for Scanning Electron Microscopy (SEM) and observing/describing specific morphological features. Terminology from Halbritter et al. (2018) and Erdtman (1969) was used.

steps.description: To observe the pollen with a scanning electron microscope (SEM), acetolyzed pollen grains were washed in ethanol
steps.searchable_summary: Wash acetolyzed pollen grains in ethanol for SEM preparation.

steps.description: and later in water.
steps.searchable_summary: Wash ethanol-washed pollen grains in water for SEM preparation.

steps.description: Grains were sputter-coated with gold
steps.searchable_summary: Sputter-coat washed pollen grains with gold for SEM.

steps.description: and observed using a Carl Zeiss EVO-50 scanning electron microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: Observe gold-coated pollen grains using a scanning electron microscope.

steps.description: Number of spines/100 μm 2 ... were described for five grains per sample.
steps.searchable_summary: Describe the number of spines per 100 μm² from SEM observations for five grains per sample.

steps.description: ...colpus end... were described for five grains per sample.
steps.searchable_summary: Describe the colpus end morphology from SEM observations for five grains per sample.

steps.description: ...base of spine... were described for five grains per sample.
steps.searchable_summary: Describe the base of spine morphology from SEM observations for five grains per sample.

steps.description: ...and pollen surface ornamentation were described for five grains per sample.
steps.searchable_summary: Describe the pollen surface ornamentation from SEM observations for five grains per sample.

methods.method_name: Pollen Data Matrix Construction and Analysis
methods.description: Procedure for structuring collected quantitative and qualitative pollen data into a matrix, calculating species averages for quantitative characters, coding qualitative characters, and visualizing character distribution.

steps.description: A matrix based on thirteen pollen characters was constructed.
steps.searchable_summary: Construct a data matrix based on thirteen pollen characters.
inputs: Raw quantitative measurement data (from light microscopy)

steps.description: To estimate the quantitative variation within Dahlia and Hidalgoa, each character was measured and the average for each species was estimated.
steps.searchable_summary: Estimate the average value for each quantitative pollen character for each species.
inputs: Quantitative data in pollen data matrix
outputs: Average values per species per quantitative character

steps.description: Qualitative characters were coded with (Table 3).
steps.searchable_summary: Code qualitative pollen characters using a defined scheme.
inputs: Qualitative data in pollen data matrix
outputs: Coded qualitative data in pollen data matrix

steps.description: Graphical representation displayed distribution of thirteen pollen characters.
steps.searchable_summary: Generate graphical representations showing the distribution of the thirteen pollen characters.
inputs: Pollen data matrix (quantitative and coded qualitative data)
